title: melica minuta subsp. angustifolia
intro: Melica minuta subsp. angustifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved melick, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Melica minuta subsp. angustifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
